rationale: Both candidates independently reach the same core insight as the ground-truth comment (submit_initial and submit_update both reject list prompts for diffusion, so dropping already_submitted is safe/correct), and X states it once, cleanly, with concrete verifying line refs and a calibrated non-blocking tone matching the actual outcome (two LGTM approvals). Y buries the same correct conclusion inside four redundant, partially self-contradictory entries on the same line (one sweep item says the unwrap is 'verified... necessary,' while two 'major/minor' items simultaneously demand the reviewer 'confirm' the very thing already verified), which hurts precision and actionability and yields a miscalibrated REQUEST CHANGES verdict for a clean, human-approved bugfix. Neither review contains a LATENT GAP CHECK section.
